preamble.summary: Isaac, now very old, calls Jacob and Esau together. He delivers final blessings and a testament. Isaac warns Esau against harboring hatred for Jacob. He adjures both sons to keep peace. Isaac divides his possessions.
preamble.remarkable: Isaac's deathbed testament follows the literary pattern of the Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs. His plea for fraternal peace carries political overtones: Judea and Edom should coexist.
preamble.friction: The emphasis on Esau's promise of peace creates dramatic irony — chapters 37-38 will narrate wars between Jacob and Esau.
preamble.connections: Genesis 35:27-29 (Isaac's death); Genesis 25:23 (oracle about the twins); Malachi 1:2-3.

verses.text_reference: And in the seventh year Isaac called his two sons Esau and Jacob.
verses.rendering: In the seventh year of this week, Isaac called his two sons Esau and Jacob. They came to him, and he said:

verses.text_reference: 'My sons, I am going the way of my fathers, to the eternal house.'
verses.rendering: My sons, I am going the way of my fathers, to the eternal dwelling where my fathers have gone.

verses.text_reference: 'Bury me near Abraham my father, in the double cave.'
verses.rendering: Bury me near my father Abraham, in the double cave in the field of Ephron the Hittite.

verses.text_reference: 'I command you to practise righteousness on the earth.'
verses.rendering: I command you, my sons: practice righteousness and integrity on the earth, so that the Lord may bring upon you everything he promised Abraham.

verses.text_reference: 'And love one another, my sons.'
verses.rendering: Love one another, my sons — love your brother as a man loves his own soul. Let each one seek how to benefit his brother.
translator_notes: Isaac's command to brotherly love anticipates Leviticus 19:18.

verses.text_reference: 'Concerning idols, I command you to reject them and hate them.'
verses.rendering: Concerning idols, I command and warn you: reject them and despise them. They are full of deception for those who worship them.

verses.text_reference: 'Remember the Lord God of Abraham your father.'
verses.rendering: Remember, my sons, the Lord God of Abraham your father, and how I too worshiped and served him in righteousness.

verses.text_reference: 'And now I shall make you swear a great oath by the Name which created the heavens and earth.'
verses.rendering: Now I will have you swear a great oath by the glorious and mighty Name that created the heavens and the earth and everything in them: that you will fear him and worship him.

verses.text_reference: 'And that each will love his brother from henceforth for ever.'
verses.rendering: And that each of you will love his brother with genuine affection and that neither will wish evil on his brother from this day forward.

verses.text_reference: 'And if either deviseth evil against his brother, he shall fall into his hand.'
verses.rendering: If either of you plots evil against his brother, know this: whoever plots evil will fall into his brother's hand and be uprooted from the land.

verses.text_reference: 'On the day of turbulence, with flaming fire as He burnt Sodom, so will He burn his land.'
verses.rendering: On the day of turmoil and fury — with a devouring fire like the one that burned Sodom — so will he burn his land, his city, and everything he has.

verses.text_reference: 'He will depart into eternal execration.'
verses.rendering: He will pass into eternal cursing, so that all judgment and curse is continually renewed against him.

verses.text_reference: 'I say and testify to you, my sons, according to the judgment which shall come.'
verses.rendering: I speak and testify to you, my sons, according to the judgment that will come upon any man who seeks to harm his brother.

verses.text_reference: And he divided all his possessions between the two on that day.
verses.rendering: That day he divided all his possessions between the two. He gave the larger portion to the firstborn.

verses.text_reference: And he said: 'This larger portion I will give to the firstborn.'
verses.rendering: He said: This larger share I give to the firstborn.

verses.text_reference: And Esau said: 'I have sold my birthright to Jacob. Let it be given to him.'
verses.rendering: Esau said: I sold my birthright to Jacob and gave it to him. Let it be given to him. I have nothing to say against it.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac said: 'May the blessing of the Lord rest upon thee, my son.'
verses.rendering: Isaac said: May the blessing of the Lord rest on you, my son, and on your offspring forever.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac called Esau and blessed him also.
verses.rendering: Isaac called his elder son Esau and said: Come here, my son, and kiss me.

verses.text_reference: And he said: 'Blessed be my son and the sons of my son. May nations serve thee.'
verses.rendering: He blessed him: Blessed be my son and his sons' sons. May the Lord give you righteous offspring. May nations serve you.

verses.text_reference: 'Be strong in the presence of men, and exercise authority over all the seed of Seth.'
verses.rendering: Be strong among men and exercise rule over all the descendants of Seth. Then your ways will be set right.

verses.text_reference: 'May the Most High God give thee all the blessings wherewith He blessed me and Noah and Adam.'
verses.rendering: May the Most High God give you all the blessings with which he blessed me and Noah and Adam.

verses.text_reference: 'May He cleanse thee from all unrighteousness.'
verses.rendering: May he cleanse you from all wickedness and forgive all the sins you have committed in ignorance.

verses.text_reference: And he blessed Jacob in like manner.
verses.rendering: He blessed Jacob in the same way: Be blessed, my son, and your sons with the blessing of the Lord forever.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac drew his feet together on to the bed, and slept the eternal sleep.
verses.rendering: Isaac drew his feet up onto the bed, turned his face, and entered the eternal sleep. He was gathered to his fathers.

verses.text_reference: And his two sons buried him in the double cave.
verses.rendering: His two sons, Esau and Jacob, buried him in the double cave near their father Abraham.

verses.text_reference: And they mourned for Isaac forty days.
verses.rendering: They mourned for Isaac forty days and were at peace.

verses.text_reference: And Esau went to the land of Edom.
verses.rendering: They parted in peace. Esau went to the land of Edom, to Mount Seir, and settled there.
